name: mask_rcnn

members.name: MaskRCNNConfig
members.signature: MaskRCNNConfig(num_classes: int = 91, in_channels: int = 3, backbone_layers: tuple[int, int, int, int] = (3, 4, 6, 3), backbone_bn_eps: float = 0.0, fpn_out_channels: int = 256, rpn_anchor_sizes: tuple[int, ...] = (32, 64, 128, 256, 512), rpn_anchor_ratios: tuple[float, ...] = (0.5, 1.0, 2.0), rpn_pre_nms_top_n: int = 1000, rpn_post_nms_top_n: int = 1000, rpn_nms_thresh: float = 0.7, rpn_min_size: float = 0.001, rpn_score_thresh: float = 0.0, rpn_fg_iou_thresh: float = 0.7, rpn_bg_iou_thresh: float = 0.3, roi_det_size: int = 7, roi_sampling_ratio: int = 2, roi_representation: int = 1024, roi_fg_iou_thresh: float = 0.5, roi_bg_iou_thresh: float = 0.5, bbox_reg_weights: tuple[float, float, float, float] = (10.0, 10.0, 5.0, 5.0), canonical_scale: int = 224, canonical_level: int = 4, roi_mask_size: int = 14, mask_hidden_channels: int = 256, mask_num_convs: int = 4, mask_predictor_hidden: int = 256, score_thresh: float = 0.05, nms_thresh: float = 0.5, max_detections: int = 100, mask_thresh: float = 0.5)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_classes: int = 91, in_channels: int = 3, backbone_layers: tuple[int, int, int, int] = (3, 4, 6, 3), backbone_bn_eps: float = 0.0, fpn_out_channels: int = 256, rpn_anchor_sizes: tuple[int, ...] = (32, 64, 128, 256, 512), rpn_anchor_ratios: tuple[float, ...] = (0.5, 1.0, 2.0), rpn_pre_nms_top_n: int = 1000, rpn_post_nms_top_n: int = 1000, rpn_nms_thresh: float = 0.7, rpn_min_size: float = 0.001, rpn_score_thresh: float = 0.0, rpn_fg_iou_thresh: float = 0.7, rpn_bg_iou_thresh: float = 0.3, roi_det_size: int = 7, roi_sampling_ratio: int = 2, roi_representation: int = 1024, roi_fg_iou_thresh: float = 0.5, roi_bg_iou_thresh: float = 0.5, bbox_reg_weights: tuple[float, float, float, float] = (10.0, 10.0, 5.0, 5.0), canonical_scale: int = 224, canonical_level: int = 4, roi_mask_size: int = 14, mask_hidden_channels: int = 256, mask_num_convs: int = 4, mask_predictor_hidden: int = 256, score_thresh: float = 0.05, nms_thresh: float = 0.5, max_detections: int = 100, mask_thresh: float = 0.5)

members.extended:
Mask R-CNN (He et al., ICCV 2017) extends Faster R-CNN with:
  1. Feature Pyramid Network (FPN) backbone replacing single-scale VGG16.
  2. RoI Align replacing RoI Pool (eliminating quantisation misalignment).
  3. A parallel mask branch predicting K binary segmentation masks per RoI.

parameters.name: num_classes
parameters.description: Foreground categories (background = class 0).

parameters.name: in_channels
parameters.description: Input image channels.

parameters.name: backbone_layers
parameters.description: ResNet layer counts (default ResNet-50 = 3,4,6,3).

parameters.name: backbone_bn_eps
parameters.description: FrozenBatchNorm2d epsilon (reference uses 0).

parameters.name: fpn_out_channels
parameters.description: Channel width of every FPN output level.

parameters.name: rpn_anchor_sizes
parameters.description: One anchor size per FPN level (P2–P6).

parameters.name: rpn_anchor_ratios
parameters.description: Aspect ratios shared across all levels.

parameters.name: rpn_pre_nms_top_n
parameters.description: Proposals kept per level before NMS.

parameters.name: rpn_post_nms_top_n
parameters.description: Proposals kept per image after NMS.

parameters.name: rpn_nms_thresh
parameters.description: RPN NMS IoU threshold.

parameters.name: rpn_min_size
parameters.description: Minimum proposal side length (feature pixels).

parameters.name: rpn_score_thresh
parameters.description: Minimum objectness score (post-sigmoid).

parameters.name: rpn_fg_iou_thresh
parameters.description: Anchor→GT IoU for foreground label.

parameters.name: rpn_bg_iou_thresh
parameters.description: Anchor→GT IoU upper bound for background.

parameters.name: roi_det_size
parameters.description: RoI Align output for detection (7 → 7×7).

parameters.name: roi_sampling_ratio
parameters.description: RoI Align sub-bin sampling ratio (2).

parameters.name: roi_representation
parameters.description: Hidden size of the 2-FC detection head.

parameters.name: roi_fg_iou_thresh
parameters.description: Proposal→GT IoU for fg assignment.

parameters.name: roi_bg_iou_thresh
parameters.description: Proposal→GT IoU upper bound for bg.

parameters.name: bbox_reg_weights
parameters.description: Per-component bbox delta scale.

parameters.name: canonical_scale
parameters.description: FPN level-assignment canonical scale (224).

parameters.name: canonical_level
parameters.description: FPN level-assignment canonical level (4).

parameters.name: roi_mask_size
parameters.description: RoI Align output for the mask branch (14 → 14×14).

parameters.name: mask_hidden_channels
parameters.description: Channel width inside the mask FCN.

parameters.name: mask_num_convs
parameters.description: Number of 3×3 convs in the mask head (4).

parameters.name: mask_predictor_hidden
parameters.description: Channel width of the deconv upsampler (256).

parameters.name: score_thresh
parameters.description: Minimum final class score.

parameters.name: nms_thresh
parameters.description: Per-class NMS IoU threshold.

parameters.name: max_detections
parameters.description: Maximum detections returned per image.

parameters.name: mask_thresh
parameters.description: Binarisation threshold applied to mask sigmoid output.

notes:
Image → ResNet-50 (C2–C5, strides 4/8/16/32)
  ↓ FPN (lateral convs + top-down merging) → [P2, P3, P4, P5, P6] (256ch)
P2–P6 → RPN → proposals
P2–P5 + proposals → FPN-level assignment → RoI Align (7×7) → 2-FC head
                                          → RoI Align (14×14) → Mask head

members.name: MaskRCNNForObjectDetection

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, proposals: list[Tensor] | None = None)
methods.summary: Run Mask R-CNN on a (pre-processed) image batch.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: (B, C, H, W) resized + normalised image batch.

parameters.name: proposals
parameters.description:
Optional precomputed per-image proposals.  When
       `None` the RPN generates them.

returns.description:
`InstanceSegmentationOutput` with raw RoI-head outputs:
`logits`     : (Σ proposals, num_classes) class logits.
`pred_boxes` : (Σ proposals, num_classes, 4) per-class boxes.
`pred_masks` : (Σ proposals, num_classes, 28, 28) mask logits.

methods.name: postprocess
methods.signature: postprocess(output: InstanceSegmentationOutput, image_sizes: list[tuple[int, int]] | None = None, proposals: list[Tensor] | None = None, features: list[Tensor] | None = None)
methods.extended:
Mirrors the reference inference flow: run the box branch
post-processing (softmax → per-class score filter → per-class NMS →
top-`max_detections`), then sigmoid the per-RoI mask logits and
gather the channel for each detection's predicted class.

parameters.name: output
parameters.description: Raw RoI-head outputs from `forward`.

parameters.name: image_sizes
parameters.description: Unused (boxes are already clipped); accepted for API symmetry.

parameters.name: proposals
parameters.description:
Per-image proposals the RoI features were sampled from
(required — pass the same list `forward` used).

parameters.name: features
parameters.description:
Multi-scale FPN feature maps; accepted for API symmetry with
the detector-stage post-processor but not consumed here (mask
logits are already gathered onto the proposals).

returns.description:
One dict per image with `"boxes"` `(D, 4)`, `"scores"`
`(D,)`, `"labels"` `(D,)` int64, and `"masks"`
`(D, 1, 28, 28)` sigmoid mask probabilities (the channel of
each detection's predicted class).

members.summary: Mask R-CNN with a ResNet-50-FPN backbone (He et al., ICCV 2017).
members.extended:
The two-stage instance-segmentation detector in its modern reference
configuration: Faster R-CNN's ResNet-50-FPN backbone, RPN, and Fast
R-CNN box head, plus a parallel FCN mask branch on the RoI heads.  The
submodule layout mirrors the reference detector so the COCO `box AP
37.9 / mask AP 34.6` checkpoint loads strict (307 keys) and reproduces
inference.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Frozen architecture spec.  Use the
`mask_rcnn_resnet50_fpn` factory for the COCO-pretrained
configuration (`num_classes = 91`).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.mask_rcnn import mask_rcnn_resnet50_fpn
>>> model = mask_rcnn_resnet50_fpn()
>>> model.eval()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape[-1]   # num_classes
91
>>> out.pred_masks.shape[-2:]
(28, 28)
notes:
See He et al., "Mask R-CNN", ICCV 2017 ([arXiv:1703.06870](https://arxiv.org/abs/1703.06870)), Ren et al.,
"Faster R-CNN", NeurIPS 2015, and Lin et al., "Feature Pyramid Networks
for Object Detection", CVPR 2017.  The model expects an already resized
+ normalised image batch; final per-instance detections + masks come
from `postprocess`.

attributes.name: config
attributes.description: Stored copy of the config that built this model.

attributes.name: backbone
attributes.description:
ResNet-50 `body` + `fpn` producing five feature maps
`[P2, P3, P4, P5, pool]` at strides `4/8/16/32/64` (reused
from Faster R-CNN).

attributes.name: rpn
attributes.description: Proposal head shared across all pyramid levels (reused).

members.name: mask_rcnn
members.signature: mask_rcnn(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: MaskRCNNResNet50FPNWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: Mask R-CNN with a ResNet-50-FPN backbone (He et al., ICCV 2017).
members.extended:
Alias of `mask_rcnn_resnet50_fpn` — the canonical reference
configuration: Faster R-CNN's ResNet-50 trunk (frozen BN) + Feature
Pyramid Network + Region Proposal Network + Fast R-CNN box head, plus
a parallel FCN mask branch.  The COCO config uses `num_classes = 91`.
Reaches COCO box AP 37.9 / mask AP 34.6 (reference
`maskrcnn_resnet50_fpn`).

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True` →
the `DEFAULT` tag (`MaskRCNNResNet50FPNWeights.COCO_V1`);
a tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over `pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides forwarded into `MaskRCNNConfig`
(`num_classes`, `rpn_pre_nms_top_n`, `score_thresh`, ...).

returns.description:
Detector with the ResNet-50-FPN configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.mask_rcnn import mask_rcnn
>>> model = mask_rcnn(num_classes=91)
>>> model.eval()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.pred_masks.shape[-2:]
(28, 28)

members.name: mask_rcnn_resnet50_fpn
members.signature: mask_rcnn_resnet50_fpn(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: MaskRCNNResNet50FPNWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: Mask R-CNN with a ResNet-50-FPN backbone (COCO-pretrained).
members.extended:
Builds the reference `maskrcnn_resnet50_fpn` detector: Faster
R-CNN's ResNet-50 backbone with frozen batch-norm, a 5-level Feature
Pyramid Network (P2-P5 + a parameter-free pool level for RPN), anchors
at sizes `((32,),(64,),(128,),(256,),(512,))` and ratios
`(0.5, 1.0, 2.0)`, a Fast R-CNN box head (TwoMLPHead +
FastRCNNPredictor) over MultiScale RoI Align (7×7), and a parallel
mask branch (MaskRCNNHeads + MaskRCNNPredictor) over MultiScale RoI
Align (14×14) emitting `28×28` per-class masks.  COCO box AP 37.9 /
mask AP 34.6, ~44.4M parameters.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Pretrained-weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True` →
the `DEFAULT` tag (`MaskRCNNResNet50FPNWeights.COCO_V1`);
a tag string → that specific checkpoint.  Mutually exclusive with
`weights` (which wins if both are given).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member.  Takes precedence over `pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides forwarded into `MaskRCNNConfig`.

returns.description:
Detector with the ResNet-50-FPN configuration applied (or with
`overrides` merged on top of it).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.mask_rcnn import mask_rcnn_resnet50_fpn
>>> model = mask_rcnn_resnet50_fpn()
>>> model.eval()
>>> out = model(lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224))
>>> out.pred_masks.shape[-2:]
(28, 28)
notes:
Pretrained weights are converted from the reference
`MaskRCNN_ResNet50_FPN_Weights.COCO_V1` checkpoint and hosted under
`lucid-dl/mask-rcnn-resnet-50-fpn`.  The detector expects an already
resized + normalised image batch (the
`lucid.utils.transforms.Detection` preset).

members.name: MaskRCNNResNet50FPNWeights
members.extended:
Single COCO checkpoint converted from the reference
`MaskRCNN_ResNet50_FPN_Weights.COCO_V1` (91 classes, box AP 37.9 /
mask AP 34.6).

summary: Mask R-CNN — instance segmentation (He et al., ICCV 2017).
extended:
Paper: "Mask R-CNN"

ResNet-50-FPN reference configuration (Faster R-CNN backbone + parallel
mask branch) with a COCO-pretrained checkpoint.
citation: He, Kaiming, et al. "Mask R-CNN." Proceedings of the IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision, 2017, pp. 2961–2969.
theory:
Mask R-CNN extends Faster R-CNN with a **parallel mask branch**
that predicts a per-RoI binary segmentation, turning the
two-stage detector into a unified instance-segmentation model
with negligible runtime overhead.

Three design changes are critical:

1. **Feature Pyramid Network backbone.**  Lateral $1 \times 1$
   convolutions fuse top-down semantically strong features with
   bottom-up spatially precise ones, yielding multi-scale outputs
   $\{P_2, \dots, P_6\}$ all at the same channel depth (256).
   Each RoI is routed to the level



$$
k = \lfloor k_0 + \log_2(\sqrt{wh}/224) \rfloor,
$$

   so small objects use high-resolution maps and large ones use
   deeper, semantically richer features.

2. **RoI Align.**  RoI Pool quantises coordinates twice
   (proposal → feature grid, then feature grid → output cells),
   which misaligns mask predictions by several pixels.  RoI Align
   removes both quantisations and bilinearly interpolates four
   regularly-sampled points per cell.  This single change adds
   $\sim 10\%$ AP on the mask task.

3. **Decoupled mask head.**  Unlike FCN-style approaches that
   compete classes via per-pixel softmax, Mask R-CNN emits a
   $K \times m \times m$ tensor of *per-class* binary masks
   and applies sigmoid + binary cross-entropy.  The classification
   branch alone chooses *which* class's mask to use at inference,
   avoiding inter-class competition during training.

The total loss is a sum of the Faster R-CNN classification +
regression terms and an additional mean BCE on the predicted
mask channel of the ground-truth class, $L = L_{\mathrm{cls}} + L_{\mathrm{box}} + L_{\mathrm{mask}}$.
